specification.topic-statement: The shared context initialization system sets up the terminal, dual database connections, tool registry, and command history in a fixed sequence, with graceful degradation at each optional step.

scope.in-scope: Terminal initialization (interactive and headless modes), stdout and stderr redirection, render system initialization, dual database connection setup, session identifier placeholder, command history loading, tool discovery and registration, internal tool registration ordering, and memory-managed cleanup registration.
scope.boundary: How tool executables are found in the filesystem and registered is handled by the tool discovery subsystem. This topic invokes discovery and provides the directory paths.
How each PostgreSQL connection is established and migrated is handled by the database connection subsystem. This topic provides the connection string and receives connection handles.
How raw mode and the alternate screen buffer are configured is handled by the terminal subsystem. This topic initiates terminal setup.

behavior.terminal-initialization:
Two paths are taken depending on whether a pre-created terminal is provided:
If a pre-created terminal handle is supplied, it is adopted as-is. Stdout and stderr are not redirected. This path is used when running without a real TTY, allowing the caller to supply a controlled terminal substitute.
If no terminal handle is supplied, a new terminal is initialized, putting the TTY into raw mode and entering the alternate screen buffer. If terminal initialization fails, the shared context is released and an error is returned.
After successful terminal initialization, stdout (file descriptor 1) and stderr (file descriptor 2) are both redirected to /dev/null. This prevents any library or system output from bypassing the alternate screen buffer and corrupting the display. The redirection is permanent for the process lifetime.
Stdout and stderr are only redirected when running interactively. Headless mode leaves the original file descriptors intact, making logging and debugging output visible to callers.
behavior.render-initialization: The render system is initialized using the terminal's screen dimensions and TTY file descriptor. If initialization fails, the terminal is cleaned up and an error is returned.
behavior.dual-database-connections:
Database connection is optional. If the configuration does not specify a host, database name, and user, both connection handles are set to null and no connection is attempted.
If the configuration is complete, a connection string is constructed and two separate connections are established:
The first connection is the main database context used for all agent-specific operations: inserting messages, updating agent state, persisting events, and running schema migrations. If this connection fails, the shared context is released and an error is returned.
The second connection is reserved for the wait synchronization system, which runs LISTEN/NOTIFY polling in a background thread. Having a separate connection prevents the blocking poll loop from interfering with main-thread database operations. If this connection fails, the shared context is released and an error is returned.
Both connections are established with the same connection string, connecting to the same database. They differ only in which subsystem uses them, not in credentials or database.
behavior.command-history-loading: The command history buffer is created and populated from the history file on disk. If loading fails, a warning is logged and initialization continues with an empty history. History loading failure does not abort startup.
behavior.tool-registration:
Tools are registered in three phases:
Executable tool files are discovered from three directories in order: the system tools directory, the user tools directory, and the project tools directory. All discovered tools are registered in the tool registry. If discovery fails, a warning is logged and initialization continues with whatever tools were registered before the failure.
Built-in internal tools are registered after external discovery completes. Because registration overwrites any existing entry with the same name, internal tools take precedence over any external tool with the same name.
The registration order is intentional. An external tool file named the same as a built-in internal tool will be replaced by the internal implementation.
After all tools are registered, the registry is sorted alphabetically by tool name. This ordering is preserved for all subsequent lookups and display.
behavior.session-identifier: The session identifier is initialized to zero. The actual session record is created later during REPL initialization, after the shared context is fully set up.
behavior.cleanup-registration: A cleanup function is registered with the memory management system. When the shared context is released, the cleanup function terminates the terminal session (restoring the original screen buffer and terminal mode) if one was established.
